 {"id":"git_safety_guard-5rbb.6","title":"[Scan] Unit tests for Dockerfile extractor","description":"# Task: Unit tests for Dockerfile extractor

## Purpose
Verify Dockerfile extractor behavior on supported syntax with deterministic unit tests.

## Requirements
- Use fixtures from Dockerfile coverage spec + edge-case fixtures
- Validate extracted commands and line/column metadata
- Assert no extraction for unsupported constructs

## Acceptance Criteria
- Unit tests cover single-line and multiline RUN
- Clear failure diagnostics with full extracted output

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-5rbb.7","title":"[Scan] Unit tests for Makefile extractor","description":"# Task: Unit tests for Makefile extractor

## Purpose
Verify Makefile extractor behavior on supported syntax with deterministic unit tests.

## Requirements
- Use fixtures from Makefile coverage spec + edge-case fixtures
- Validate extracted commands, line numbers, and recipe handling
- Assert no extraction for unsupported constructs

## Acceptance Criteria
- Unit tests cover tabs, continuations, and comments
- Clear failure diagnostics

-{"id":"git_safety_guard-5rbb.9","title":"[Scan] Add SARIF 2.1.0 output format","description":"# Task: Add SARIF 2.1.0 Output Format for Scan Mode

## Background

SARIF (Static Analysis Results Interchange Format) is the industry standard for security scanning output. It integrates natively with:
- GitHub Code Scanning
- GitLab Code Quality
- Azure DevOps
- VS Code SARIF Viewer extension
- SonarQube

Adding SARIF support positions DCG as a first-class citizen in CI/CD security pipelines.

## Implementation

### CLI Integration

```
dcg scan . --format sarif > results.sarif
dcg scan . --format sarif --output results.sarif
```

### SARIF 2.1.0 Structure

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oasis-tcs/sarif-spec/main/sarif-2.1/schema/sarif-schema-2.1.0.json",
  "version": "2.1.0",
  "runs": [{
    "tool": {
      "driver": {
        "name": "dcg",
        "version": "2.0.0",
        "informationUri": "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/destructive_command_guard",
        "rules": [
          {
            "id": "git.force-push",
            "name": "Force Push Detection",
            "shortDescription": { "text": "Detects git push --force" },
            "fullDescription": { "text": "..." },
            "defaultConfiguration": { "level": "error" },
            "helpUri": "https://dcg.dev/rules/git/force-push"
          }
        ]
      }
    },
    "results": [
      {
        "ruleId": "git.force-push",
        "level": "error",
        "message": { "text": "Destructive git force push detected" },
        "locations": [{
          "physicalLocation": {
            "artifactLocation": { "uri": ".github/workflows/ci.yml" },
            "region": {
              "startLine": 45,
              "startColumn": 9,
              "endLine": 45,
              "endColumn": 35
            }
          }
        }],
        "fixes": [{
          "description": { "text": "Remove --force flag" },
          "artifactChanges": [{
            "artifactLocation": { "uri": ".github/workflows/ci.yml" },
            "replacements": [{
              "deletedRegion": { "startLine": 45, "startColumn": 20, "endColumn": 28 },
              "insertedContent": { "text": "" }
            }]
          }]
        }]
      }
    ]
  }]
}
```

### Key Files

- src/sarif.rs - New: SARIF output formatting
- src/scan.rs - Add sarif format option
- src/cli.rs - Wire up --format sarif

### Rust Implementation

```rust
// src/sarif.rs
use serde::Serialize;

#[derive(Serialize)]
pub struct SarifReport {
    #[serde(rename = "$schema")]
    pub schema: String,
    pub version: String,
    pub runs: Vec<SarifRun>,
}

#[derive(Serialize)]
pub struct SarifRun {
    pub tool: SarifTool,
    pub results: Vec<SarifResult>,
}

impl SarifReport {
    pub fn from_scan_results(findings: Vec<ScanFinding>) -> Self {
        // Convert DCG findings to SARIF format
    }
}
```

### Severity Mapping

| DCG Severity | SARIF Level | Security Score |
|--------------|-------------|----------------|
| critical     | error       | 9.0-10.0       |
| high         | error       | 7.0-8.9        |
| medium       | warning     | 4.0-6.9        |
| low          | note        | 0.1-3.9        |

## GitHub Actions Integration

```yaml
name: Security Scan
on: [push, pull_request]

jobs:
  dcg-scan:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Install DCG
        run: curl -fsSL https://dcg.dev/install.sh | bash
      
      - name: Run DCG scan
        run: dcg scan . --format sarif --output results.sarif
      
      - name: Upload SARIF
        uses: github/codeql-action/upload-sarif@v3
        with:
          sarif_file: results.sarif
```

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (tests/sarif_test.rs)

```rust
#[test]
fn test_sarif_schema_compliance() {
    log::info\!("=== Testing SARIF 2.1.0 schema compliance ===");
    
    let findings = vec\![ScanFinding::mock_high_severity()];
    let sarif = SarifReport::from_scan_results(findings);
    let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&sarif).unwrap();
    
    log::debug\!("Generated SARIF:\n{}", json);
    
    // Validate against schema
    let schema = load_sarif_schema();
    let instance: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&json).unwrap();
    let result = schema.validate(&instance);
    
    assert\!(result.is_ok(), "SARIF must validate against schema");
    log::info\!("=== SARIF schema compliance test PASSED ===");
}

#[test]
fn test_sarif_severity_mapping() {
    log::info\!("=== Testing severity to level mapping ===");
    
    let mappings = [
        (Severity::Critical, "error"),
        (Severity::High, "error"),
        (Severity::Medium, "warning"),
        (Severity::Low, "note"),
    ];
    
    for (severity, expected_level) in mappings {
        let finding = ScanFinding::with_severity(severity);
        let sarif = SarifReport::from_scan_results(vec\![finding]);
        let level = &sarif.runs[0].results[0].level;
        
        log::debug\!("Severity {:?} -> Level {}", severity, level);
        assert_eq\!(level, expected_level);
    }
    
    log::info\!("=== Severity mapping test PASSED ===");
}
```

### E2E Tests (tests/e2e/sarif_e2e.sh)

```bash
#\!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

LOG_FILE="e2e_sarif_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).log"
exec > >(tee -a "$LOG_FILE") 2>&1

echo "=== DCG SARIF E2E Test Suite ==="
DCG="./target/release/dcg"

# Create test files
TEMP_DIR=$(mktemp -d)
echo "RUN rm -rf /" > "$TEMP_DIR/Dockerfile"
echo "git push --force origin main" > "$TEMP_DIR/deploy.sh"

# Test 1: Generate SARIF
echo "[1/4] Testing SARIF generation..."
$DCG scan "$TEMP_DIR" --format sarif > "$TEMP_DIR/results.sarif"
if jq -e ".version == \"2.1.0\"" "$TEMP_DIR/results.sarif" > /dev/null; then
    echo "  ✓ SARIF 2.1.0 version correct"
else
    echo "  ✗ FAILED: Version mismatch"
    exit 1
fi

# Test 2: Schema validation
echo "[2/4] Validating against SARIF schema..."
# Using ajv or similar JSON schema validator
if command -v ajv &> /dev/null; then
    ajv validate -s sarif-schema-2.1.0.json -d "$TEMP_DIR/results.sarif"
    echo "  ✓ Schema validation passed"
else
    echo "  ⚠ ajv not installed, skipping schema validation"
fi

# Test 3: Results contain expected fields
echo "[3/4] Verifying result structure..."
if jq -e ".runs[0].results[0].ruleId" "$TEMP_DIR/results.sarif" > /dev/null; then
    echo "  ✓ Results have ruleId"
fi
if jq -e ".runs[0].results[0].locations[0].physicalLocation" "$TEMP_DIR/results.sarif" > /dev/null; then
    echo "  ✓ Results have location info"
fi

# Test 4: Tool info present
echo "[4/4] Verifying tool information..."
TOOL_NAME=$(jq -r ".runs[0].tool.driver.name" "$TEMP_DIR/results.sarif")
if [[ "$TOOL_NAME" == "dcg" ]]; then
    echo "  ✓ Tool name is dcg"
else
    echo "  ✗ FAILED: Unexpected tool name: $TOOL_NAME"
    exit 1
fi

rm -rf "$TEMP_DIR"

echo "=== All SARIF E2E Tests Passed ==="
```

## Success Criteria

- SARIF 2.1.0 output validates against official schema
- GitHub Code Scanning integration works
- All findings include file, line, column info
- Rules array populated with all triggered patterns
- Fix suggestions included when available
- Detailed logging in tests

## Dependencies

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-62v","title":"[CATEGORY] email.* - Email Service Pack Suite","description":"# Email Service Pack Suite

## Overview
Email services handle transactional and marketing communications. Domain verification deletion breaks sending. Template deletion affects automated emails.

## Packs to Implement

### email.sendgrid
- **Why Critical**: SendGrid handles high-volume transactional email. API key or domain deletion breaks email flow.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `sendgrid api_keys delete`
  - `sendgrid templates delete`
  - Domain authentication removal
  - Suppression list operations
- **Safe Patterns**: `list`, `get`

### email.ses
- **Why Critical**: AWS SES is deeply integrated with AWS applications.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `aws ses delete-identity`
  - `aws ses delete-template`
  - `aws ses delete-configuration-set`
  - `aws sesv2 delete-email-identity`
- **Safe Patterns**: `list-`, `get-`, `describe-`

### email.mailgun
- **Why Critical**: Mailgun handles developer-focused email delivery.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - Domain deletion
  - Route deletion
  - Credential deletion
  - Mailing list deletion
- **Safe Patterns**: `list`, `get`, `info`

### email.postmark
- **Why Critical**: Postmark focuses on transactional email reliability.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - Server deletion
  - Template deletion
  - Domain removal
- **Safe Patterns**: `list`, `get`

## Dependencies
- Depends on: Tier 3 Epic (git_safety_guard-9ic)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Major email providers covered
- [ ] Domain/identity deletion detected
- [ ] Template deletion handled
- [ ] API key operations flagged","notes":"Testing/Validation:
- Every email.* pack must comply with Standard Pack Task Requirements (git_safety_guard-ikf2), including unit tests and e2e corpus scenarios.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-64dc","title":"[PERF][P0] Startup latency: lazy pack registry + lazy regex compilation","description":"# Goal

Make the **common allow path** (commands that should pass quickly, e.g. `ls`, `cd`, `rg`) fast again by eliminating the current dominant startup cost: eager pack construction + eager regex compilation.

This is **pure performance work**. It must be **provably isomorphic**: for the same input JSON, the allow/deny decision (including `pack_id`, `pattern_name`, reason strings, fail-open behavior, and ordering/tie-breaking) must not change.

# Why this matters (real numbers)

dcg runs once per Bash command. If the hook costs ~175ms, that is catastrophic: it makes every command feel sluggish.

Baseline (measured 2026-01-09, using `./target/release/dcg`):

## Correctness baseline
- `cargo test` → pass
- `cargo build --release`
- `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --binary ./target/release/dcg` → **pass** (211/211)

## Process-per-command latency baseline (warmup 30, measure 300)
Measured by looping the release binary and piping hook JSON on stdin.

- `DCG_BYPASS=1` (returns after config load, *before packs*):
  - p50 ≈ **1.55ms**, throughput ≈ **643/s**

- Normal hook allow/deny cases (`ls`, `git status`, deny cases, heredoc-ish cases):
  - p50 ≈ **173–179ms**, throughput ≈ **5.6–5.8/s**

Key isolate:
- `./target/release/dcg config`: p50 ≈ **1.58ms**
- `./target/release/dcg packs --enabled`: p50 ≈ **177.5ms**

This strongly implies: **pack registry initialization dominates**.

## Peak memory baseline
Using `/usr/bin/time -v`:
- Hook run: Max RSS ≈ **15.5MB**
- `DCG_BYPASS=1`: Max RSS ≈ **4.7MB**
- `dcg packs --enabled`: Max RSS ≈ **14.0MB**

# Profile before proposing (done; top hotspots)

`perf` was blocked on this host (`perf_event_paranoid=4`), so profiling used **gprofng**.

CPU + heap profiles show:
- `destructive_command_guard::packs::PackRegistry::new` dominates.
- Inside it, `fancy_regex::Regex::new` + compilation/determinization dominates.
- Heap tracing shows extremely high allocation volume in regex compilation.

Artifacts captured under `/tmp` (not in repo):
- `/tmp/dcg_packs_loop_cpu.er`
- `/tmp/dcg_packs_debug.er`

# Root cause (current architecture)

`REGISTRY` initializes a `PackRegistry` that constructs **all packs** and eagerly compiles **all regexes** (including packs that are not enabled for the current run, and even when the command is quick-rejectable).

This makes even trivial allow commands pay the full regex compilation cost.

# Strategy (high-level)

1. **Make pack registry metadata-only** (pack ids, tier, keywords, pattern *strings*).
2. **Defer regex compilation until actually needed**:
   - Parse hook JSON to get `command` first.
   - Run keyword gate using metadata-only keyword sets.
   - Only if needed: compile regexes for the enabled packs that might match.
3. Keep semantics isomorphic:
   - Pack ordering remains stable.
   - Safe-before-destructive logic unchanged.
   - Allowlists and config overrides unchanged.
   - Fail-open behavior unchanged.

# Success criteria / perf budgets (post-change targets)

These targets are intentionally aggressive because current overhead is dominated by avoidable work.

- `dcg` hook allow case (no enabled pack keywords):
  - p50 ≤ **5ms**, p95 ≤ **10ms**, p99 ≤ **20ms** (release build)

- `dcg packs --enabled`:
  - p50 ≤ **5ms** (should be metadata-only)

- Max RSS:
  - allow case ≤ **6MB**

# Regression guardrails

- Add tests that assert "no regex compilation on quick-reject allow path" (via a `#[cfg(test)]` compile counter in the regex engine).
- Add a repeatable perf harness that records p50/p95/p99 + throughput and stores JSON artifacts in-repo for diffing.
- CI should fail if allow-path budgets regress.

# Non-goals

- Changing policies (WARN vs DENY) or reducing false positives by altering rules.
- Introducing daemons or changing operational semantics.
- Broad refactors not directly tied to startup/compile elimination.

Lazy pack registry + lazy regex compilation achieved 56x improvement (175ms→3.1ms p50). All 287 e2e tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-64dc","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:12:47.468632316-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-64dc.1","title":"[DESIGN] PackSpec + LazyPattern architecture (no eager regex compile)","description":"# Objective

Design an internal representation for packs/patterns that allows:

- Listing packs and keywords **without compiling regex**.
- Running the hook allow path (quick reject) **without compiling regex**.
- Compiling regex **only** for the enabled packs that are actually evaluated.

This must be **isomorphic** to current behavior.

# Background / evidence

Baseline + profiling show startup is dominated by:
- `packs::PackRegistry::new`
- `fancy_regex::Regex::new` compilation

Even `dcg packs --enabled` costs ~177ms (release) because it triggers registry construction.

# Constraints (must preserve)

- Pack ordering + tie-breaking remains deterministic (existing stable order behavior).
- Safe-before-destructive evaluation is preserved.
- `pack_id`/`pattern_name` attribution remains stable.
- Fail-open semantics remain unchanged.
- No new false positives/negatives.

# Proposed representation

## Pack metadata (`PackSpec`)

A `PackSpec` must contain only:
- `pack_id: &'static str`
- `tier: Tier` (or ordering key)
- `keywords: &'static [&'static str]`
- `safe_patterns: &'static [SafePatternSpec]`
- `destructive_patterns: &'static [DestructivePatternSpec]`
- Any pack-specific knobs that are strictly metadata (no compiled state)

## Pattern specs (store *strings*, compile lazily)

### `SafePatternSpec`
- `name: &'static str`
- `pattern: &'static str`
- `compiled: OnceLock<CompiledRegex>` (or `OnceLock<fancy_regex::Regex>` initially)

### `DestructivePatternSpec`
- `name: Option<&'static str>`
- `pattern: &'static str`
- `reason: &'static str`
- `compiled: OnceLock<CompiledRegex>`

API sketch:
- `fn is_match(&self, haystack: &str) -> bool`
- `fn find_match_span(&self, haystack: &str) -> Option<(usize, usize)>` (needed for explainability / matched-span output)

Compilation behavior:
- Compiled on first use (per-process), using `OnceLock::get_or_init`.

# Handling regex compilation errors (isomorphism considerations)

Current code uses `.expect("pattern should compile")` at pack construction time, which would crash early if a pattern is invalid.

With lazy compilation, compile failures would surface on first use. This changes *when* the panic happens (not the allow/deny output on valid builds).

To keep "invalid patterns are caught immediately" as a property **without paying compile cost in production**:

- Add a **unit test** `all_pack_patterns_compile` that compiles every pattern once (under `#[cfg(test)]`).
- Optionally add `dcg packs --validate` to compile everything explicitly (debug-only / developer command).

This maintains strong correctness while still letting production avoid compilation on the quick allow path.

# Where the architecture must integrate

- `src/main.rs` hook path must be able to compute:
  - enabled pack ids
  - keyword union
  - pack order

…without forcing regex compilation.

- `dcg packs --enabled` must list packs without compilation.

# Acceptance criteria

- No `Regex::new` calls occur during registry init for allow-case commands.
- `dcg packs --enabled` becomes metadata-only and fast.
- Design explicitly documents:
  - which modules/structs change
  - how deterministic ordering is preserved
  - how match spans are extracted without double-compiling

# Deliverable

A short design note (can live directly in this bead as updates/comments) that commits to:
- concrete types (`PackSpec`, pattern spec structs, lazy compile primitive)
- migration plan (phase-by-phase)
- explicit isomorphism proof sketch

-//! SARIF 2.1.0 output format for scan results.
-//!
-//! SARIF (Static Analysis Results Interchange Format) is the industry standard
-//! for security scanning output. It integrates natively with:
-//! - GitHub Code Scanning
-//! - GitLab Code Quality
-//! - Azure DevOps
-//! - VS Code SARIF Viewer extension
-//! - SonarQube
-//!
-//! Reference: <https://docs.oasis-open.org/sarif/sarif/v2.1.0/sarif-v2.1.0.html>
-
-use crate::scan::{ScanDecision, ScanFinding, ScanReport, ScanSeverity};
-use serde::Serialize;
-use std::collections::HashMap;
-
-/// SARIF 2.1.0 schema URI.
-pub const SARIF_SCHEMA: &str =
-    "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oasis-tcs/sarif-spec/main/sarif-2.1/schema/sarif-schema-2.1.0.json";
-
-/// SARIF version string.
-pub const SARIF_VERSION: &str = "2.1.0";
-
-/// DCG tool information URI.
-pub const DCG_INFO_URI: &str = "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/destructive_command_guard";
-
-/// Top-level SARIF report structure.
-#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]
-#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
-pub struct SarifReport {
-    /// JSON schema for SARIF 2.1.0.
-    #[serde(rename = "$schema")]
-    pub schema: String,
-
-    /// SARIF version (always "2.1.0").
-    pub version: String,
-
-    /// Analysis runs (typically one per tool invocation).
-    pub runs: Vec<SarifRun>,
-}
-
-/// A single analysis run.
-#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]
-#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
-pub struct SarifRun {
-    /// Tool that produced this run.
-    pub tool: SarifTool,
-
-    /// Results (findings) from this run.
-    pub results: Vec<SarifResult>,
-
-    /// Invocation details.
-    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
-    pub invocations: Option<Vec<SarifInvocation>>,
-}
-
-/// Tool information.
-#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]
-#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
-pub struct SarifTool {
-    /// Driver (primary tool component).
-    pub driver: SarifToolComponent,
-}
-
-/// Tool component (driver or extension).
-#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]
-#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
-pub struct SarifToolComponent {
-    /// Tool name.
-    pub name: String,
-
-    /// Tool version.
-    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
-    pub version: Option<String>,
-
-    /// Semantic version.
-    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
-    pub semantic_version: Option<String>,
-
-    /// URI for tool information.
-    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
-    pub information_uri: Option<String>,
-
-    /// Rules defined by this tool.
-    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Vec::is_empty")]
-    pub rules: Vec<SarifReportingDescriptor>,
-}
-
-/// Reporting descriptor (rule definition).
-#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]
-#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
-pub struct SarifReportingDescriptor {
-    /// Unique rule identifier.
-    pub id: String,
-
-    /// Rule name (human-readable).
-    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
-    pub name: Option<String>,
-
-    /// Short description.
-    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
-    pub short_description: Option<SarifMessage>,
-
-    /// Full description.
-    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
-    pub full_description: Option<SarifMessage>,
-
-    /// Help URI for this rule.
-    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
-    pub help_uri: Option<String>,
-
-    /// Default configuration.
-    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
-    pub default_configuration: Option<SarifReportingConfiguration>,
-
-    /// Properties bag for additional metadata.
-    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
-    pub properties: Option<SarifPropertyBag>,
-}
-
-/// Reporting configuration.
-#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]
-#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
-pub struct SarifReportingConfiguration {
-    /// Default severity level.
-    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
-    pub level: Option<SarifLevel>,
-
-    /// Whether the rule is enabled by default.
-    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
-    pub enabled: Option<bool>,
-}
-
-/// SARIF severity level.
-#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize)]
-#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
-pub enum SarifLevel {

-    /// Warning.
-    Warning,
-    /// Error.
-    Error,
-}
-
-impl From<ScanSeverity> for SarifLevel {
-    fn from(severity: ScanSeverity) -> Self {
-        match severity {
-            ScanSeverity::Error => SarifLevel::Error,
-            ScanSeverity::Warning => SarifLevel::Warning,
-            ScanSeverity::Info => SarifLevel::Note,
-        }
-    }
-}

-/// A message with text content.

-            text: text.into(),
-            markdown: None,
-        }
-    }
-}

-#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]

-
-    /// Code flows (execution paths).
-    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Vec::is_empty")]
-    pub code_flows: Vec<SarifCodeFlow>,
-
-    /// Suggested fixes.
-    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Vec::is_empty")]
-    pub fixes: Vec<SarifFix>,
-
-    /// Properties bag.
-    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
-    pub properties: Option<SarifPropertyBag>,
-}
-
-/// Location in a source file.
-#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]
-#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
-pub struct SarifLocation {
-    /// Physical location in a file.
-    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]

-
-    /// Message describing this location.
-    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
-    pub message: Option<SarifMessage>,
-}
-
-/// Physical location (file + region).
-#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]
-#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
-pub struct SarifPhysicalLocation {

-impl SarifReport {

-                    full_description: None,
-                    help_uri: Some(format!(
-                        "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/destructive_command_guard/blob/master/docs/rules/{}.md",
-                        rule_id.replace(':', "/").replace('.', "/")
-                    )),
-                    default_configuration: Some(SarifReportingConfiguration {
-                        level: Some(finding.severity.into()),
-                        enabled: Some(true),
-                    }),
-                    properties: None,
-                };
-                rules_map.insert(rule_id, rule);
-            }
-        }
-
-        let rules: Vec<_> = rules_map.into_values().collect();
-
-        // Convert findings to results
-        let results: Vec<_> = report
-            .findings
-            .iter()
-            .filter(|f| f.decision != ScanDecision::Allow) // Only include warns/denies
-            .map(|f| finding_to_result(f))
-            .collect();
-
-        Self {
-            schema: SARIF_SCHEMA.to_string(),
-            version: SARIF_VERSION.to_string(),
-            runs: vec![SarifRun {
-                tool: SarifTool {
-                    driver: SarifToolComponent {
-                        name: "dcg".to_string(),
-                        version: Some(version.to_string()),
-                        semantic_version: Some(version.to_string()),
-                        information_uri: Some(DCG_INFO_URI.to_string()),
-                        rules,
-                    },
-                },
-                results,
-                invocations: Some(vec![SarifInvocation {
-                    execution_successful: true,
-                    working_directory: std::env::current_dir().ok().map(|p| SarifArtifactLocation {
-                        uri: p.display().to_string(),
-                        uri_base_id: None,
-                    }),
-                    start_time_utc: None,
-                    end_time_utc: None,
-                }]),
-            }],
-        }
-    }
-}
-
-/// Convert a scan finding to a SARIF result.
-fn finding_to_result(finding: &ScanFinding) -> SarifResult {
-    let rule_id = finding
-        .rule_id
-        .clone()
-        .unwrap_or_else(|| finding.extractor_id.clone());
-
-    let level = match finding.decision {
-        ScanDecision::Deny => SarifLevel::Error,
-        ScanDecision::Warn => SarifLevel::Warning,
-        ScanDecision::Allow => SarifLevel::Note,
-    };
-
-    let message = finding
-        .reason
-        .clone()
-        .unwrap_or_else(|| format!("Destructive command detected: {}", finding.extracted_command));
-
-    let mut properties = SarifPropertyBag::new();
-    properties.insert("extractor_id", &finding.extractor_id);
-    properties.insert("extracted_command", &finding.extracted_command);
-    properties.insert("decision", format!("{:?}", finding.decision));
-
-    // Build location
-    let location = SarifLocation {
-        physical_location: Some(SarifPhysicalLocation {
-            artifact_location: SarifArtifactLocation {
-                uri: finding.file.clone(),
-                uri_base_id: Some("%SRCROOT%".to_string()),
-            },
-            region: Some(SarifRegion {
-                start_line: Some(finding.line),
-                start_column: finding.col,
-                end_line: Some(finding.line),
-                end_column: None,
-                snippet: Some(SarifArtifactContent {
-                    text: finding.extracted_command.clone(),
-                }),
-            }),
-        }),
-        message: None,
-    };
-
-    // Build fix suggestion if available
-    let fixes = finding.suggestion.as_ref().map_or_else(Vec::new, |s| {
-        vec![SarifFix {
-            description: SarifMessage::text(s),
-            artifact_changes: vec![],
-        }]
-    });
-
-    SarifResult {
-        rule_id,
-        level,
-        message: SarifMessage::text(message),
-        locations: vec![location],
-        code_flows: vec![],
-        fixes,
-        properties: Some(properties),
-    }
-}
-
-/// Convert a rule ID like "git.force-push" to "Git Force Push".
-fn humanize_rule_id(rule_id: &str) -> String {
-    rule_id
-        .split(|c| c == '.' || c == ':' || c == '-' || c == '_')
-        .map(|word| {
-            let mut chars: Vec<char> = word.chars().collect();
-            if let Some(first) = chars.first_mut() {
-                *first = first.to_ascii_uppercase();
-            }
-            chars.into_iter().collect::<String>()
-        })
-        .collect::<Vec<_>>()
-        .join(" ")
-}
-
-#[cfg(test)]
-mod tests {
-    use super::*;
-    use crate::scan::{ScanDecision, ScanFinding, ScanReport, ScanSeverity, ScanSummary};
-
-    fn mock_finding(decision: ScanDecision, severity: ScanSeverity) -> ScanFinding {
-        ScanFinding {
-            file: "Dockerfile".to_string(),
-            line: 23,
-            col: Some(5),
-            extractor_id: "dockerfile.run".to_string(),
-            extracted_command: "rm -rf /".to_string(),
-            decision,
-            severity,
-            rule_id: Some("core.filesystem:recursive-delete-root".to_string()),
-            reason: Some("Recursively deletes the entire filesystem".to_string()),
-            suggestion: Some("Use a specific path instead of root".to_string()),
-        }
-    }
-
-    fn mock_report() -> ScanReport {
-        ScanReport {
-            schema_version: 1,
-            summary: ScanSummary {
-                files_scanned: 5,
-                files_skipped: 0,
-                commands_extracted: 2,
-                findings_total: 2,
-                decisions: Default::default(),
-                severities: Default::default(),
-                max_findings_reached: false,
-                elapsed_ms: None,
-            },
-            findings: vec![
-                mock_finding(ScanDecision::Deny, ScanSeverity::Error),
-                mock_finding(ScanDecision::Warn, ScanSeverity::Warning),
-            ],
-        }
-    }
-
-    #[test]
-    fn test_sarif_schema_compliance() {
-        let report = mock_report();
-        let sarif = SarifReport::from_scan_report(&report);
-
-        assert_eq!(sarif.version, "2.1.0");
-        assert!(sarif.schema.contains("sarif-schema-2.1.0"));
-        assert_eq!(sarif.runs.len(), 1);
-    }
-
-    #[test]
-    fn test_sarif_tool_info() {
-        let report = mock_report();
-        let sarif = SarifReport::from_scan_report(&report);
-
-        let driver = &sarif.runs[0].tool.driver;
-        assert_eq!(driver.name, "dcg");
-        assert!(driver.version.is_some());
-        assert!(driver.information_uri.is_some());
-    }
-
-    #[test]
-    fn test_sarif_results_exclude_allow() {
-        let mut report = mock_report();
-        report.findings.push(mock_finding(ScanDecision::Allow, ScanSeverity::Info));
-
-        let sarif = SarifReport::from_scan_report(&report);
-
-        // Should only include Deny and Warn, not Allow
-        assert_eq!(sarif.runs[0].results.len(), 2);
-    }
-
-    #[test]
-    fn test_sarif_severity_mapping() {
-        assert_eq!(SarifLevel::from(ScanSeverity::Error), SarifLevel::Error);
-        assert_eq!(SarifLevel::from(ScanSeverity::Warning), SarifLevel::Warning);
-        assert_eq!(SarifLevel::from(ScanSeverity::Info), SarifLevel::Note);
-    }
-
-    #[test]
-    fn test_sarif_location_info() {
-        let report = mock_report();
-        let sarif = SarifReport::from_scan_report(&report);
-
-        let result = &sarif.runs[0].results[0];
-        assert!(!result.locations.is_empty());
-
-        let loc = &result.locations[0];
-        let phys = loc.physical_location.as_ref().unwrap();
-        assert_eq!(phys.artifact_location.uri, "Dockerfile");
-
-        let region = phys.region.as_ref().unwrap();
-        assert_eq!(region.start_line, Some(23));
-        assert_eq!(region.start_column, Some(5));
-    }
-
-    #[test]
-    fn test_sarif_rules_populated() {
-        let report = mock_report();
-        let sarif = SarifReport::from_scan_report(&report);
-
-        let rules = &sarif.runs[0].tool.driver.rules;
-        assert!(!rules.is_empty());
-        assert!(rules
-            .iter()
-            .any(|r| r.id == "core.filesystem:recursive-delete-root"));
-    }
-
-    #[test]
-    fn test_sarif_fix_suggestions() {
-        let report = mock_report();
-        let sarif = SarifReport::from_scan_report(&report);
-
-        let result = &sarif.runs[0].results[0];
-        assert!(!result.fixes.is_empty());
-        assert!(result.fixes[0]
-            .description
-            .text
-            .contains("specific path"));
-    }
-
-    #[test]
-    fn test_sarif_json_valid() {
-        let report = mock_report();
-        let sarif = SarifReport::from_scan_report(&report);
-
-        let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&sarif).unwrap();
-        assert!(json.contains("\"version\": \"2.1.0\""));
-        assert!(json.contains("\"$schema\""));
-        assert!(json.contains("\"runs\""));
-    }
-
-    #[test]
-    fn test_humanize_rule_id() {
-        assert_eq!(humanize_rule_id("git.force-push"), "Git Force Push");
-        assert_eq!(
-            humanize_rule_id("core.filesystem:recursive-delete"),
-            "Core Filesystem Recursive Delete"
-        );
-        assert_eq!(humanize_rule_id("docker_system_prune"), "Docker System Prune");
-    }
-}
